title: The Control of National Highways (Land and Traffic) Act, 2002
full_text: The Control of National Highways (Land and Traffic) Act, 2002 राष्‍ट्रीय राजमार्ग नियंत्रण (भूमि और यातायात)अधिनियम, 2002 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 200313 Act Number: 13 Enactment Date: 2003-01-14 Act Year: 2003 Short Title: The Control of National Highways (Land and Traffic) Act, 2002 Hindi Title: राष्‍ट्रीय राजमार्ग नियंत्रण (भूमि और यातायात)अधिनियम, 2002 Long Title: An Act to provide for control of land within the National Highways, right of way and traffic moving on the National Highways and also for removal of unauthorised occupation thereon. Ministry: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways Enforcement Date: 27-01-2005 Notification: 27th January, 2005, vide notification No. S.O. 96(E) dated 27th January, 2005, see Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II sec. 3(ii). TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Control of National Highways (Land and Traffic) Act, 2002 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPTER II HIGHWAYS ADMINISTRATION CHAPTER III PREVENTION OF UNAUTHORISED OCCUPATION OF HIGHWAY LAND AND THEIR REMOVAL CHAPTER IV CONTROL OF ACCESS TO THE NATIONAL HIGHWAYS CHAPTER V REGULATION OF DIFFERENT TYPES OF TRAFFIC ON NATIONAL HIGHWAYS CHAPTER VI REGULATION OF CONSTRUCTION ON HIGHWAY LAND FOR PUBLIC UTILITIES, DRAINS, ETC. CHAPTER VII OFFENCE AND PENALTY CHAPTER VIII MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title, extent and commencement. Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Establishment of Highway Administrations. Show Related Subordinates Section 4. Powers and functions of Highway Administration. Section 5. Establishment of Tribunals. Show Related Subordinates Section 6. Omitted. Show Related Subordinates Section 7. Omitted. Section 8. Omitted. Section 9. Omitted. Section 10. Omitted. Section 11. Omitted. Section 12. Omitted. Section 13. Omitted. Section 14. Appeal. Show Related Subordinates Section 15. Bar of jurisdiction. Section 16. Procedure and powers of Tribunal. Section 17. Conditions as to making of interim order. Section 18. Execution of orders of Tribunal. Section 19. Limitation. Show Related Subordinates Section 20. Appointment of officers to act on behalf of Highway Administration. Section 21. Delegation of powers. Section 22. Power to transfer jurisdiction. Show Related Subordinates Section 23. Highway land to be deemed as property of Central Government. Show Related Subordinates Section 24. Prevention of occupation of highway land. Show Related Subordinates Section 25. Grant of lease or licence of highway land for temporary use. Section 26. Removal of unauthorised occupation. Show Related Subordinates Section 27. Recovery of cost of removal of unauthorised occupation and fine imposed. Show Related Subordinates Section 28. Right of access. Show Related Subordinates Section 29. Procedure for permission to access to Highway. Show Related Subordinates Section 30. Regulation or diversion of access, etc. Section 31. Highway Administration to regulate traffic when Highway deemed unsafe. Section 32. Prohibition of use of heavy vehicles on certain Highways. Show Related Subordinates Section 33. Temporary closure of traffic on Highway. Section 34. Permanent closure of Highway. Section 35. Power to restrict the use of vehicles. Show Related Subordinates Section 36. Prevention and repair of damage to Highway. Section 37. Prohibition to leave vehicles or animals in dangerous position. Show Related Subordinates Section 38. Construction on highway land. Show Related Subordinates Section 39. Offence and penalty. Section 40. Right of appellant to take assistance of legal practitioner. Section 41. Finality of orders. Section 42. Duty of village officials. Section 43. Conduct of inquiry. Show Related Subordinates Section 44. Presiding Officers and employees of the Tribunals, etc., to be public servants. Section 45. Protection of action taken in good faith. Section 46. Offences by companies. Section 47. Procedure for service of notices, etc. Show Related Subordinates Section 48. Act to have overriding effect. Section 49. Power to remove difficulties. Section 50. Power to make rules.
